commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=90ba2c0fd87fe7f455fd855df73a958d0c05776e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR backfills missing Codex visible-account reset/window metadata from scoped cached snapshots and adds regression coverage for account/source boundary cases.

Reproducibility: Source-level yes: current main’s visible-account refresh stores labeled Codex snapshots without backfilling missing reset metadata, and the PR body describes focused regression shapes. I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a narrow identity-guard repair and contributor-supplied real behavior proof; automation should not request repair markers while the external proof gate remains unmet.

target.title: Preserve account reset timestamps
target.body:
Fixes #1283

## Summary
- Backfill missing Codex reset/window metadata before persisting visible per-account usage snapshots.
- Scope cached reset metadata to the same visible account source and identity so same-email siblings, source switches, and reused visible IDs do not inherit stale reset times.
- Add regressions for stacked visible accounts and the single-card account-switch path.

## Root cause
The selected/global refresh path already retained missing reset timestamps from the last known snapshot before updating state. The per-account visible refresh path labeled the account snapshot and stored it immediately, so a fresh response that omitted `resetsAt` or `windowMinutes` could persist without the known 5-hour and weekly window metadata.

The first fix repaired the matching-account case, but it still needed stricter account/source guards so cached reset metadata could not leak across same-email visible accounts or after an account source transition.

## Real Proof
PR: https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/pull/1298

Minimal reproduction shape covered locally:
- Stacked visible account refresh where the fresh active account response has `primary.windowMinutes = 0`, `primary.resetsAt = nil`, and no secondary window, while the matching cached account has a 300-minute primary reset and a 10080-minute secondary reset.
- Same-email sibling account, newly active same-email account, collapsed same-email visible ID, reused email ID with different selection source, changed live visible ID, different-email active cache, single-card live-to-managed same-email source switch, undated secondary window, and stale/expired reset-description cases.

Positive proof from the focused regression:
- Fresh matching account response: primary `usedPercent = 1`, `windowMinutes = 0`, `resetsAt = nil`, secondary `nil`.
- Cached matching account: primary `windowMinutes = 300`, future session reset, reset text `"4:06 PM"`; secondary `windowMinutes = 10080`, future weekly reset, reset text `"next week"`.
- Stored visible account snapshot after the fix: primary keeps fresh `usedPercent = 1` and gains `windowMinutes = 300`, the future session reset, and `"4:06 PM"`; secondary is promoted with `windowMinutes = 10080`, the future weekly reset, and `"next week"`.
- Selected/main snapshot receives the same repaired matching-account metadata instead of reapplying the unscoped global fallback.

Negative proof from focused regressions:
- Same-email sibling, newly active same-email account, collapsed same-email ID, reused ID with different source, changed live visible ID, different-email active cache, and single-card same-email source switch all keep `primary.resetsAt == nil` instead of copying stale cached reset metadata.
- Account-scoped invalidation clears `lastKnownResetSnapshots[.codex]` so a live-to-managed same-email switch in single-card mode cannot reuse the previous source's reset cache.
- A cached secondary window without a future reset date is not promoted.
- A stale cached reset description is not copied when the fresh response has a different reset date; expired cached reset text is also dropped.

Earlier provider proof feasibility: local CLI was installed and authenticated. A redacted live `--all-accounts` refresh returned reset metadata through the installed account path:

```json
{
  "source": "codex-cli",
  "usage": {
    "accountEmail": "[redacted-email]",
    "primary": {
      "resetsAt": "2026-06-04T08:32:42Z",
      "usedPercent": 43,
      "windowMinutes": 300
    },
    "secondary": {
      "resetsAt": "2026-06-11T03:32:42Z",
      "usedPercent": 7,
      "windowMinutes": 10080
    }
  }
}
```

## Checks
- `swift test --filter CodexAccountResetBackfill` passed: 11 tests.
- `swift test --filter ResetTimeBackfillTests` passed: 5 tests.
- `swift test --filter UsageStorePlanUtilizationCodexOwnershipTests` passed: 12 tests.
- `swift build` passed.
- `make check` passed: SwiftFormat reported 0/1001 files needing formatting; SwiftLint reported 0 violations in 1000 files.
- `CLANG_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/private/tmp/codexbar-clang-module-cache SWIFT_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/private/tmp/codexbar-swift-module-cache swift test --filter 'CodexAccountResetBackfillTests|ResetTimeBackfillTests'` passed: 16 focused tests.

I also ran the full `swift test` earlier; it hit one unrelated timing failure in `CodexBackgroundRefreshCoalescingTests.swift:43` (`rapid regular refreshes coalesce concurrent Codex credits fetches`). The focused touched-area suites above pass consistently.

Remaining risk: proof is bounded to deterministic UsageStore/RateWindow tests and the redacted local CLI/account path; no live credentials or provider transcripts are included.
target.author: rohitjavvadi
